title: 1.1: Removes the SetMain(src, c) call from the newBundle function in js/bundle.go

annotations.description: Verifies that the two-step requirement (SetMain then RunMain) is removed. The module system should work without requiring SetMain to be called first.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 9-11 show removal of the SetMain call from bundle.go; lines 43-51 show removal of the SetMain method from ModuleResolver.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the 'two-step requirement' of calling SetMain before RunMain 'makes the module system inflexible' and should be removed. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The current design requires SetMain to initialize state before RunMain works. [3: Logical Necessity] To remove the coupling, calling SetMain must no longer be necessary. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must eliminate the requirement to call SetMain before running modules.

title: 1.2: Implements RunSourceData method on ModuleSystem in js/modules/resolution.go to run source data directly without requiring prior initialization

annotations.description: Verifies that the module system provides a RunSourceData method to run source data directly at execution time, without requiring prior setup via SetMain.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 89-104 show the RunSourceData method on ModuleSystem accepting *loader.SourceData and returning (goja.Value, error).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the goal is 'run source data directly without requiring prior initialization'. The interface specifies RunSourceData method on *ModuleSystem with input *loader.SourceData and output goja.Value, error. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The existing RunMain method cannot run source directly as it relies on state set by SetMain. [3: Logical Necessity] To run source directly, a mechanism must accept source as input. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must implement RunSourceData.

title: 1.3: Updates js/bundle.go to call RunSourceData instead of RunMain

annotations.description: Verifies that bundle.go is updated to use the new RunSourceData method instead of the old RunMain method.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 19-20 show the change from modSys.RunMain() to modSys.RunSourceData(b.sourceData) in bundle.go.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires removing the coupling between SetMain and RunMain. The interface defines RunSourceData as the replacement. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] bundle.go is the consumer that calls RunMain after SetMain. [3: Logical Necessity] After replacing RunMain with RunSourceData, the caller must be updated. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must update bundle.go to call RunSourceData.

title: 1.4: Implements resolveLoaded method on ModuleResolver in js/modules/resolution.go to resolve and cache modules from pre-loaded data

annotations.description: Verifies that a resolveLoaded helper method is implemented to resolve and cache modules from pre-loaded byte data.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 60-73 show the resolveLoaded method on ModuleResolver accepting basePWD, arg, and data parameters.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The interface specifies resolveLoaded as a method on *ModuleResolver with input (basePWD *url.URL, arg string, data []byte) and output (module, error). [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] RunSourceData needs a way to compile and cache source data without going through the file-loading resolve path. [3: Logical Necessity] The existing resolve method loads from files; a new method is needed for pre-loaded data. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must implement resolveLoaded.

title: 2.1: Adds documentation for RunSourceData explaining its behavior (doc comments covering caching semantics or parameter handling)

annotations.description: Verifies that the direct source execution capability has documentation explaining its behavior.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 83-86 show doc comments explaining the direct execution behavior including caching semantics.
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] When changing execution behavior, documentation helps users understand how to use the new capability. [4: Quality Necessity] Good documentation improves code maintainability. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer should document the direct execution behavior.

title: 3.1: Removes both internal state fields (mainpwd and mainSpecifier) used by SetMain from the ModuleResolver struct in js/modules/resolution.go

annotations.description: Verifies cleanup of the internal state fields that were only needed for the SetMain/RunMain coupling.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 32-35 show removal of mainpwd and mainSpecifier fields from ModuleResolver struct.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The mainpwd and mainSpecifier fields exist solely to support the SetMain/RunMain coupling. [4: Quality Necessity] After removing SetMain, these fields become dead code. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any good answer should remove these unused fields.

title: 3.2: Removes the SetMain method from ModuleResolver in js/modules/resolution.go

annotations.description: Verifies cleanup of the SetMain method after the coupling is removed.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 43-51 show removal of the SetMain method.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] SetMain is the method that creates the coupling the prompt asks to remove. [4: Quality Necessity] After the refactoring, SetMain is dead code. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any good answer should remove the SetMain method.
